scenarios.raw: Steel frame with masonry infill walls
scenarios.reasoning: Steel structural frame governs; masonry infill is non-structural

scenarios.raw: CMU walls with wood roof joists
scenarios.reasoning: Combustible wood roof joists over masonry walls forces Joisted Masonry classification

scenarios.raw: Concrete frame with glass curtain wall
scenarios.reasoning: Concrete structural frame governs; glass curtain wall is non-structural facade

scenarios.raw: Tilt-up concrete walls with steel roof deck
scenarios.reasoning: Tilt-up concrete walls govern structural classification; steel roof deck is secondary

scenarios.raw: Wood frame with brick veneer
scenarios.reasoning: Wood structural frame governs; brick veneer is cosmetic/non-structural

scenarios.raw: Steel frame with wood mezzanine
scenarios.reasoning: Steel primary frame governs; wood mezzanine is minor secondary element, not load-bearing for the primary system

scenarios.raw: Precast walls with steel frame
scenarios.reasoning: Steel structural frame governs; precast panels act as exterior cladding

scenarios.raw: Concrete podium with wood upper floors
scenarios.reasoning: Wood structural upper floors are combustible; concrete podium + wood upper = Mixed Construction

scenarios.raw: Heavy timber with masonry walls
scenarios.reasoning: Heavy timber is the primary structural system; masonry walls are secondary

scenarios.raw: Metal building with masonry front facade
scenarios.reasoning: Metal rigid frame governs; masonry front facade is decorative/non-structural

scenarios.raw: Concrete shear walls with steel roof truss
scenarios.reasoning: Concrete shear walls are primary structural system; steel roof truss is secondary

scenarios.raw: Steel frame retrofitted with concrete infill
scenarios.reasoning: Original steel frame governs; concrete infill is a retrofit addition

scenarios.raw: Unreinforced masonry with steel roof
scenarios.reasoning: URM load-bearing walls govern (weakest system); steel roof is non-structural secondary

scenarios.raw: CMU lower level with wood upper level
scenarios.reasoning: Combustible wood upper level structure over CMU lower = Mixed Construction

scenarios.raw: Steel warehouse with attached wood office
scenarios.reasoning: Two distinct structural systems (steel + wood) in connected occupancy = conservative Mixed

scenarios.raw: Pre-engineered metal warehouse with interior wood office
scenarios.reasoning: Metal primary structure governs; interior wood office is a non-structural partition

scenarios.raw: Concrete parking garage attached to steel office
scenarios.reasoning: Two distinct structural systems sharing risk but not separable → conservative Unknown/Mixed

scenarios.raw: Steel moment frame with precast panels
scenarios.reasoning: Steel moment frame is structural system; precast panels are cladding only

scenarios.raw: Masonry exterior with steel interior framing
scenarios.reasoning: Steel interior structural frame governs; masonry exterior is facade

scenarios.raw: Wood truss roof over masonry walls
scenarios.reasoning: Masonry load-bearing walls + combustible wood truss roof = Joisted Masonry

scenarios.raw: 5-story wood over concrete podium
scenarios.reasoning: Combustible wood structural upper floors present over concrete podium = Mixed

scenarios.raw: Steel frame high-rise with concrete core
scenarios.reasoning: Steel perimeter frame governs; concrete core is secondary lateral system

scenarios.raw: Concrete building with steel canopy
scenarios.reasoning: Concrete primary structure governs; steel canopy is a non-structural appendage

scenarios.raw: Brick exterior, unknown frame
scenarios.reasoning: Only facade known, structural frame unknown → conservative Joisted Masonry downgrade

scenarios.raw: Metal building with partial masonry addition
scenarios.reasoning: Masonry addition creates different structural system from metal primary → Mixed

scenarios.raw: Wood framing with reinforced concrete foundation
scenarios.reasoning: Wood structural frame governs; concrete foundation is not part of the structural classification

scenarios.raw: Reinforced concrete lower floors, wood penthouse
scenarios.reasoning: Combustible wood penthouse addition above RC structure creates vertical hybrid = Mixed

scenarios.raw: Steel warehouse with concrete block office extension
scenarios.reasoning: Two different structural systems in connected occupancies → conservative Mixed

scenarios.raw: CMU walls reinforced with steel columns
scenarios.reasoning: CMU is dominant load system; steel columns provide reinforcement → Masonry Non-Combustible

scenarios.raw: Concrete tilt-up with wood interior framing
scenarios.reasoning: If wood interior framing is load-bearing, downgrade from Tilt-Up to Mixed Construction

combustible_downgrade.description: Combustible roof or floor joists on masonry walls forces Joisted Masonry

urm_governs_weakest.description: URM load-bearing walls always govern regardless of secondary elements

mixed_construction.description: Two distinct load-bearing structural systems or vertical hybrid triggers Mixed

tilt_up_governs.description: Tilt-up structural walls govern over any roof type

heavy_timber_governs.description: Heavy timber as primary structural system governs over masonry secondary

unknown_frame_conservative_downgrade.description: When only facade material is known and frame is unknown, downgrade conservatively to Joisted Masonry

minor_combustible_allowed.description: Minor non-load-bearing combustible element does not trigger downgrade if primary frame is non-combustible

primary_governs_interior_partition.description: A non-structural interior wood partition does not change the primary classification

_meta.source: 30-scenario structural conflict analysis table — insurance underwriting construction classification
_meta.notes: Scenario 17 maps to AIR 100 (Unknown) when two distinct non-combustible systems cannot be prioritized. All other Mixed use AIR 141.
